Article Title: 'Anti-Ambedkar' BJP used money power, deployed ‘agent RO’ to defeat dalit activist in Haryana Rajya Sabha: Congress
Article Text:
Former Haryana chief minister Bhupinder Singh Hooda, with Karamvir Singh Boudh, who was elected as Rajya Sabha MP from Haryana



Watch NDA Storm In Rajya Sabha Polls Bihar Clean Sweep Odisha Wins Haryana On Hold NDA Storm In Rajya Sabha Polls Bihar Clean Sweep Odisha Wins Haryana On Hold

NEW DELHI: Congress on Tuesday said the governing BJP employed “money power, threats and misuse of returning officer” to win the Haryana Rajya Sabha election at any cost, as it slammed the rival for again “exposing its anti-Ambedkar character” by trying to steal the parliament membership of grassroots Dalit activist Karamvir Boudh.After scraping a victory in the Haryana RS polls, despite cross voting by five Congress MLAs and four of its votes declared invalid, Congress MP Deepender Hooda said BJP had appointed Gujarat deputy CM as the observer for the polls which showed that the BJP leadership wanted to win the election irrespective of the tactic used.“BJP tried to murder democracy in the dark of night. All the four votes declared as invalid were completely valid.They had even been put in the tray of valid votes by an official. But this was done by a blatantly partisan RO (returning officer) to contrive the numbers in favour of BJP,” he told a press conference with Congress’s scheduled castes department chairman Rajendra Pal Gautam.Deepender said Congress will take strong action against the MLAs who cross voted and betrayed the party, adding that people will not forgive the legislators who betrayed their mandate.He said BJP wanted to engineer cross-voting by nine or seven Congress legislators while securing two votes of INLD. He said INLD, “which is B-team of BJP”, declared its decision to abstain from polling barely half an hour before the voting ended, because it was sure that Congress was winning the election.He said when BJP’s “game of pressure and money” failed, BJP employed its third strategy of fixing the play through RO. “RO worked as a BJP agent,” he added.
Article Summary: Congress slams rival for trying to steal parliament membership of grassroots Dalit activist Karamvir Boudh. Congress MP Deepender Hooda said BJP had appointed Gujarat deputy CM as the observer for the polls. He said BJP wanted to engineer cross-voting by nine or seven Congress legislators.

Article Title: Pradyut Bordoloi: Assam Congress MP Pradyut Bordoloi resigns ahead of state polls
Article Text:
Watch ECI Announces Assembly Election Dates For West Bengal, Assam, Tamil Nadu, Kerala & Puducherry ECI Announces Assembly Election Dates For West Bengal, Assam, Tamil Nadu, Kerala & Puducherry

Poll What do you think influenced Pradyut Bordoloi's decision to resign from the Congress party? Pressure from rivals Upcoming elections strategy

NEW DELHI: Assam senior Congress leader and Lok Sabha MP Pradyut Bordoloi on Tuesday resigned from the party just 20 days, ahead of the state assembly elections.Bordoloi submitted his resignation to AICC president Mallikarjun Kharge, party sources told PTI.In his brief, one-line resignation letter addressed to Kharge, Bordoloi wrote, "With an overwhelming sense of sadness today, I hereby tender my resignation from all posts, privileges and the primary membership of the Indian National Congress ."A former Assam cabinet minister and a two-time MP from the Nagaon constituency, Bordoloi is a senior Congress leader in the state.His son is currently contesting the upcoming assembly elections as a Congress candidate from the Margherita seat, where voting is scheduled for April 9.Following the development, Assam Pradesh Congress Committee chief Gaurav Gogoi said that, "I have come after talking to him, and I condemn the kind of things that are coming out in the media."
Article Summary: Assam senior Congress leader and Lok Sabha MP Pradyut Bordoloi on Tuesday resigned from the party just 20 days, ahead of the state assembly elections. His son is currently contesting the upcoming assembly elections as a Congress candidate from the Margherita seat.

Article Title: West Bengal elections: EC transfers 19 senior police officers in fresh reshuffle ahead of polls
Article Text:
The IPS officers were transferred as ordered by the Election Commission of India.

Nandigram Redux As BJP Pits Suvendu Adhikari Against Mamata Banerjee From Bhabanipur In Bengal Polls

NEW DELHI: The Election Commission of India (ECI) on Tuesday ordered the transfer of 19 senior police officers in West Bengal, marking the third consecutive day of such reshuffles in the poll-bound state.The poll body replaced the ADG/IGPs of the south and north Bengal regions, the police commissioners of Howrah, Barrackpore, Chandannagar, and Asansol-Durgapur, as well as 12 superintendents of police (SPs) and a deputy commissioner (DC) across 13 districts.The first round of transfers was carried out in the state’s top bureaucracy just hours after the ECI announced the assembly election schedule on Sunday for four states, including West Bengal, and the Union territory of Puducherry.The second round of transfers took place on Monday afternoon within the senior ranks of the West Bengal Police. The ECI is empowered to transfer officers directly involved in elections under Article 324 of the Constitution.Under it, the poll body has the power of "superintendence, direction, and control" over the entire poll machinery in a state or Union territory during elections.However, the transfers drew sharp criticism in Parliament from the Trinamool Congress (TMC), which governs West Bengal. TMC MP Derek O’Brien announced a day-long walkout by party MPs from the Rajya Sabha to protest what he called “transfers made by the ECI in the middle of the night.” Parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju, however, emphasised that the poll panel was exercising its constitutional mandate and accused the TMC of "routinely attacking constitutional bodies. "All 294 assembly constituencies in West Bengal will be held in two phases - April 23 and 29 - with voting for 152 and 142 seats, respectively. The counting will be held on May 4.
Article Summary: The Election Commission of India (ECI) on Tuesday ordered the transfer of 19 senior police officers in West Bengal. The poll body replaced the ADG/IGPs of the south and north Bengal regions, the police commissioners of Howrah, Barrackpore, Chandannagar, and Asansol-Durgapur. The first round of transfers was carried out in the state’s top bureaucracy just hours after the ECI announced the assembly election schedule.

Article Title: Bjp Kerala First List: Rajeev Chandrasekhar, Kurian make BJP’s first list for Kerala
Article Text:
Kerala BJP president and NDA candidate from Nemom constituency Rajeev Chandrasekhar during a roadshow in Thiruvananthapuram

Kerala Assembly Polls: Left’s Final Fortress at Stake As LDF Tries To Hold On Kerala Bastion

THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: BJP on Monday announced its first list of 47 candidates for the Kerala assembly election, with Union minister George Kurian to fight from Kanjirappally, while the party’s state president Rajeev Chandrasekhar will contest from Nemom seat and former Union minister V Muraleedharan will once again seek mandate from Kazhakkoottam.K Surendran, former state president of the party, will contest from Manjeswar, a seat he lost by a whisker in 2021. Former DGP and Sasthamangalam corporation ward councillor R Sreelekha has been fielded from Vattiyoorkavu, while BJP state vice-president C Krishnakumar will contest from Malampuzha. Sobha Surendran has been given the Palakkad seat while R Reshmi, former state general secretary of Mahila Congress who switched to BJP last month, will contest from Kottarakkara.The BJP central election committee, chaired by party president Nitin Nabin, finalised the candidates for the 47 seats at a meeting that was also attended by PM Modi and Union minister Amit Shah. Other candidates named in the first list include A P Gangadharan (Payyannur), C Raghunath (Kannur), Kavitha A S (Sultan Bathery - ST), Navya Haridas (Kozhikode North), T Raneesh (Kozhikode South), Padmaja Venugopal (Thrissur), A Ajith (Vaikom - SC), B B Gopakumar (Chathannur), P Sudheer (Attingal), Yuvaraj Gokul (Nedumangad).
Article Summary: BJP announces its first list of 47 candidates for the Kerala assembly election. Union minister George Kurian to fight from Kanjirappally. State president Rajeev Chandrasekhar will contest from Nemom seat. Former Union minister V Muraleedharan will once again seek mandate from Kazhakkoottam.

Article Title: Mangaluru man rams Innova into scooter in bid to kill bizman over Rs 2 crore land dispute
Article Text:
Mangaluru East Police have arrested Mohammad Sharif for allegedly attempting to murder Mohan Das Shetty





MANGALURU: The Mangaluru East Police have arrested a man for allegedly attempting to murder a person by ramming his car into a two-wheeler following a dispute over a land deal.The accused is Mohammad Sharif, 26, from Falnir, Mangaluru.According to the police, Mohan Das Shetty, 56, a resident of Mallikatte, had a financial dispute with the accused, Mohammad Sharif, and his father, Mohammad Iqbal, regarding a land transaction.It is alleged that the accused invested around Rs 2 crore in properties at Kana in Surathkal and Vamanjoor, and suspected that the complainant had cheated him in the deal.On Wednesday morning, the accused met the complainant near Arya Samaj Road under the pretext of a discussion and allegedly snatched his mobile phone.Later, when the complainant was riding his two-wheeler, the accused, in a fit of rage, allegedly drove his SUV at high speed and rammed into the two-wheeler from behind in an attempt to kill him.Based on the complaint, a case has been registered at Mangaluru East Police Station under sections 109, 281, 352, 351, 304(2) of the BNS. The accused will be produced before a court.
Article Summary: Mohan Das Shetty, 56, had a financial dispute with the accused, Mohammad Sharif, and his father, Mohammad Iqbal, regarding a land transaction. It is alleged that the accused invested around Rs 2 crore in properties at Kana in Surathkal and Vamanjoor and suspected that the complainant had cheated him in the deal.
